title: ADP timeline ROW (layer 2)
description: Schema for ONE row of the 1 Hz UTC canonical timeline. In practice the timeline is stored column-wise as parquet (see schemas/README.md); this schema documents the per-row/per-column contract. Rows are on a strict 1 s UTC grid spanning the union of all sources. The ONLY required field is time_utc: every telemetry column is nullable/optional because a given second may predate a source, fall in a dropout, or be a filled/interpolated slot.

time_utc.description: Row timestamp on the 1 Hz UTC grid (ISO 8601, UTC). The one guaranteed field.

lat.description: Canonical latitude (WGS84). NULLABLE: null where no source had a fix this second (dropout or pre/post source span).

lon.description: Canonical longitude (WGS84). NULLABLE, same as lat.

moving.description: Moving/stationary flag for this second. Nullable where undetermined.

grit.description: Garmin Grit — vendor-special metric promoted to a bare canonical column.

flow.description: Garmin Flow — vendor-special metric promoted to a bare canonical column.

^[a-z0-9]+_raw$.description: Per-source row-level presence flag: true = this second is a real sample from <source>, false = interpolated/filled/absent. E.g. garmin_raw, bosch_raw.

^[a-z0-9]+_(lat|lon)$.description: Per-source shadow GPS column preserving <source>'s own fix even when canonical lat/lon picked another source. E.g. bosch_lat, bosch_lon.

^[a-z0-9]+_(alt_m|speed_ms|hr_bpm|power_.description: Per-source shadow metric column (<source>_<metric>) preserving the original per-source value. Units are fixed in the suffix (_m, _ms, _bpm, _w, _rpm). E.g. bosch_hr_bpm, garmin_alt_m, bosch_speed_ms.

$comment: additionalProperties is true because the canonical set plus per-source shadow/flag columns is open-ended across devices; patternProperties document and constrain the <source>_<metric>, <source>_<lat|lon>, and <source>_raw families. Only time_utc is required.
